description: §39.12 positive control — a well-formed `<schema>` block placed correctly as an immediate child of `<program db="./app.db">`, declaring a single `users` table with a primary key and legal column types (`integer`, `text`) plus recognized constraints (`primary key`, `not null`, `unique`). This is the canonical §39.2 valid shape; the whole E-SCHEMA-* family stays silent — pinned via `notCodePrefixes` so any future spurious member of the family fails this case.
rationale: §39.12 defines the E-SCHEMA-* diagnostic family for malformed schema declarations (missing `db=`, duplicate blocks, misplacement, unknown column types, bad defaults/references/renames). A conforming `<schema>` block — correctly placed, with a primary key and only legal column types + constraints — fires none of them. This case asserts family-glob absence of the E-SCHEMA- prefix as the soundness backstop for the negative schema case.
